architecture.summary: LaSR is a training paradigm for Speech LLMs that internalizes chain-of-thought reasoning without explicit token generation. During training, audio is encoded as tokens and progressively input to an LLM backbone. The method structures a causal reasoning trajectory: unlabeled latent reasoning over initial audio tokens, timestamp-aligned explicit CoT supervision around the target terminology region (determined by forced alignment), unlabeled latent reasoning after CoT, and finally transcription supervision. The model learns to perform internal reasoning at these key acoustic stages while generating only the final transcription output, achieving context-aware ASR without additional inference latency.
trained_components: Speech LLM backbone (via latent reasoning trajectory supervision)

biblio.title: LaSR: Context-Aware Speech Recognition via Latent Reasoning

claims.dataset: Spoken Darwin-Science evaluation set (2000 real-world scientific speech segments)
claims.statement: LaSR with CoT start point 0.50s before terminology anchor achieves hard set EER decrease of 7.74 percentage points and overall EER reduction of 4.14 percentage points (20.32% relative improvement) compared to base Fun-Audio-Chat-8B model
Qwen3-ASR-0.6B with supervised fine-tuning on 20% of Spoken Darwin-Science achieves hard set EER decrease from 42.93% to 33.33% (22.36% relative improvement)
LaSR does not introduce additional latency compared to standard SFT because it does not require token-by-token prefilling during decoding

On Qwen3-ASR-1.7B, LaSR training with latent reasoning produces interference and underperforms non-thinking SFT across all metrics (WER 5.83% vs SFT 5.34%; all EER categories degrade), indicating LaSR requires sufficient model capacity and reasoning capability

Autoregressive CoT (explicit generation of reasoning before transcription) on Fun-Audio-Chat-8B significantly degrades performance, with base set EER increasing from 12.68% to 18.83% and WER increasing from 6.25% to 7.93%, while hard set improvement does not compensate
Human verification of 1000 synthesized terminology samples shows 96.8% pronunciation accuracy according to fluent English speakers using online dictionaries as reference

Spoken Darwin-Science consists of 2.7M instances (with 20% subset of 539K having CoT annotations) filtered from 440B tokens of academic papers across nine scientific domains, with 2000 real-world evaluation samples from YouTube lectures, TED talks, and documentaries

LaSR differs from prior latent reasoning approaches by establishing fixed time-aligned anchor points within the acoustic signal rather than progressively transforming explicit CoT tokens into latent reasoning, allowing flexible emergent reasoning paths while maintaining temporal structure
